channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Kamala Harris claims she's got a gun, but Second Amendment supporters say good luck getting yours
item.description: Kamala Harris recently drew the ire of Second Amendment advocates after claiming she owned a "Glock," which contrasts with her previous support for bans and restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Things to know about AP's report on the federal criminal cases against former President Donald Trump
item.description: A year that began with the prospect of a federal court reckoning for Donald Trump will conclude without any chance at trial, leaving voters without an up-or-down jury verdict in the two most consequential cases against the Republican presidential nominee.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democratic voter registration raises red flags for Harris
item.description: Democrats’ voter registration advantage has dropped in three key battleground states — Pennsylvania, North Carolina and Nevada — raising a red flag for Vice President Harris as experts cite a lack of enthusiasm for the Biden administration brand and the Democratic Party, generally, as problems.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Vance doesn't say where patients will get care if Planned Parenthood is defunded
item.description: Ohio Sen. JD Vance (R), former President Trump's running mate, on Sunday didn't say where patients would go for health screenings, checkups and more if Planned Parenthood is defunded.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Scully, NewsNation

item.title: Unknown drone fleet breached US military base airspace in Virginia for 17 straight days: report
item.description: An unknown fleet of unmanned aircraft swarmed Langley Air Force Base in Virginia for 17 consecutive days late last year, the Wall Street Journal reported.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walz tramps through tall grass but bags no birds as pheasant hunting season opens
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Democratic vice presidential candidate Tim Walz carried a shotgun and tramped through tall grass as Minnesota's pheasant hunting season opened, giving the campaign a chance to highlight the governor's rural roots and love of outdoor sports. Neither Walz nor Lt. Gov.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inside the TikTok documents: Stripping teens and boosting ‘attractive’ people
item.description: A trove of secret documents show teens’ increasingly reliant on TikTok and how executives were acutely aware of the potential harm the app can cause young people, but appeared unconcerned.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, Sylvia Goodman, and Dara Kerr, MPR News

item.title: This year's 'October surprise' may be no surprise at all
item.description: It is not uncommon for political commentators to call a late-breaking story during a presidential election an "October surprise" — even when there is little evidence that it mattered all that much.
item.dc:creator: Ron Elving, MPR News

item.title: Donald Trump holds a rally in California, a state he's almost certain to lose
item.description: COACHELLA, Calif. (AP) — With the presidency on the line in battlegrounds like Wisconsin and Pennsylvania, Donald Trump spent Saturday night in solidly liberal California, seeking to link Vice President Kamala Harris to what he described as the failures of her home state.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meghan McCain calls out Kamala Harris over remarks about late Arizona senator: 'Don't make me start'
item.description: Meghan McCain criticized Vice President Kamala Harris after the Democratic candidate claimed late Sen. John McCain once told her she would be a "great" senator.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance points out the 'biggest difference' between leadership of Trump, Harris during campaign stop
item.description: At a campaign stop in Pennsylvania on Saturday, Sen. JD Vance called out VP Kamala Harris for her failures to America's homeless veterans during her tenure in the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Poll workers, poll watchers - here is the difference
item.description: Two components are poll workers and poll watchers. Election Assistance Commissioner Benjamin Hovland says both are essential to ensuring our elections run smoothly and fairly.
item.dc:creator: Marlo Lacen, NewsNation

item.title: Tameka Henry discusses oversight, safety, and leadership in Clark County School District race
item.description: Tameka Henry, a candidate for the Clark County Board of Trustees, appeared on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to discuss her platform and vision for improving the school district.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson discusses government transparency, public safety, and housing
item.description: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide Friday to discuss issues she believes need to be addressed in the city.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jamie Dimon: 'Treacherous' geopolitical conditions getting worse
item.description: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon on Friday warned about potential risks in the future for the economy, citing the war in Ukraine and Israel's war against Hamas and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Biden suggests Trump 'get a life' after false hurricane claims
item.description: Biden specially called out a claim that those impacted by the storm are only receiving $750 in government assistance.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: The drownings of 2 Navy SEALs were preventable, military investigation finds
item.description: The highly critical report concluded there were “deficiencies, gaps and inconsistencies” in training and policies.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden suggests Trump 'get a life' after false hurricane claims
item.description: “Mr. President Trump, former President Trump, get a life man, help these people,” Biden said in remarks on Hurricane Milton from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Annette Dawson Owens discusses education, healthcare, and affordable housing
item.description: Annette Dawson Owens, a Republican candidate for Nevada’s Assembly District 29, recently discussed her views on education, healthcare, and affordable housing on 8 News Now’s Daily Voting Guide.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Candidate Kamilah Bywaters discusses her vision for the Clark County School Board
item.description: Kamilah Bywaters, a candidate for the Clark County School District (CCSD) Board of Trustees, joined 8 News Now’s Daily Voting Guide to discuss her plans and priorities for District E, which covers Summerlin and northwest parts of the Las Vegas Valley.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ryan Hamilton discusses homelessness, the Animal Foundation, and F1
item.description: Ryan Hamilton, a Republican candidate for the Clark County Commission, appeared on 8 News Now's Daily Voting Guide to share his perspective on homelessness, animal welfare, and Formula One's impact on the local economy as the election nears.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pat Ryan, Alison Esposito face off in NY-18 debate on PIX11
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Democratic U.S. Rep. Pat Ryan and Republican Alison Esposito, the two candidates running to represent New York's 18th Congressional District, faced off in a debate on PIX11 Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: The presidential campaigns have made over 200 visits but speak to just a fraction of American voters
item.description: The nation's unique Electoral College system for electing a president, which replaces the popular vote, puts disproportionate voting power in the hands of a relative few states.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden postpones trip to Germany and Angola to monitor Hurricane Milton
item.description: President Joe Biden said Tuesday he is postponing a planned trip to Germany and Angola to stay at the White House to monitor Hurricane Milton, as it spins closer to Florida’s Gulf Coast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida has nearly all ballots counted on Election Day, while California can take weeks. This is why
item.description: Why is it that some states have nearly all the ballots counted within hours of polls closing while others take weeks?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Harris leads Trump among California Latino voters by 23 points
item.description: Vice President Harris holds a strong lead over former President Trump among California Hispanics, according to a Telemundo poll released Tuesday. The California survey contrasts with a similar poll released earlier this month of Florida Hispanic voters, which found Trump leading Harris.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, Fox 5

item.title: Your guide to voting in Minnesota: Registration, absentee ballots and general election details
item.description: We are less than a month out from Election Day. Here are answers to some frequently asked questions MPR News readers asked us about the upcoming election.
item.dc:creator: Anika Besst and Nicole Johnson, MPR News

item.title: Harris is pressed for more specifics in a wide-ranging interview with '60 Minutes'
item.description: The interview followed criticism from former President Donald Trump that Kamala Harris has been unwilling to sit for hard interviews. She was pressed on everything from her economic proposals to immigration.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: GOP senator calls Trump special counsel filing 'a temper tantrum from a deranged fanatic'
item.description: Arkansas Sen. Tom Cotton (R) said in a Sunday interview that a recently unsealed filing from special counsel Jack Smith is “a temper tantrum from a deranged fanatic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: This year’s Electoral College map may show another ‘blue shift.’ Here’s why
item.description: Differences in how quickly each state’s counties can report this year’s election results may lead to another “blue shift” or “red mirage” on the presidential electoral map.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News
